A discussion with the presumed pilot aligned with the aircrafts recorded fight data. The pilot described the most probable landing as less than optimal and was not concern based on several similar past landings of which did not result in an accelerometer upload event. With the pilotÂ’s account of events and lack of onboard flight data to support the WiFi upload mechanism/protocol, the validity of the Wi-Fi reporting system would appear to be in this instance questionable; no further action was taken.

action: System maintained retract position for two hours without a pump activation. Purged main gear pistons of old hydraulic fluid. Vacuumed pump reservoir fluid and refilled with new fluid. Cycled new fluid through main lines via the bypass valve and numerous cycles (no unsafe condition). Pump reservoir fluid discolored again.

problem: Pilot stated: "Right main gear hydraulic failure. Recycled and fixed itself but happened twice on the flight."
action: Flushed/bleed entire retract hydraulic system/hoses/pistons/bypass valve and pump reservoir with new fluid. Cycled gear numerous times until reservoir level stabilized in the correct range. Cleaned pressure switch connector contacts. System maintained retract pressure for 1+ hour without a pump activation. Re-installed MX meter fuse, closed/secured nose bowl and removed from jacks.
